title: Campylandra delavayi
taxonomy: Campylandra delavayi is classified as a species within the genus Campylandra, which belongs to the family Asparagaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf and flower morphology. The plant is further categorized under the family Asparagaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Campylandra is itself part of the Asparagales order, which consists of various plant families with similar characteristics. Campylandra delavayi's classification is subject to ongoing research and potential revisions.

intro: Campylandra delavayi, also known as Delavay's Campylandra, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Campylandra delavayi include saponins, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Campylandra delavayi is often prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in capsule or tablet form to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
